title: Strong Password Checker
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal challenge: the single recommended solution is also the speed extreme and the memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.cpp is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: The problem is a short, branch-heavy string scan with scalar counters. Native C++ avoids interpreter and GC overhead, uses direct ASCII comparisons, and needs no auxiliary containers, making it the fastest practical and memory-conscious choice among the allowed LeetCode languages for this workload.
variants.approachJustification: Count missing character classes and repeated-run replacement pressure. For overlength passwords, spend mandatory deletions in the optimal len % 3 priority order to reduce replacements, then combine remaining replacement pressure with missing type repairs. This same O(n), O(1) strategy is also the speed and memory extreme.

reasoningSummary: A run of length L needs L / 3 replacements unless deletions shrink it. Under the mandatory n - 20 deletions for overlong passwords, deleting from runs with L % 3 == 0 saves a replacement after one deletion, L % 3 == 1 after two deletions, and all remaining saves cost three deletions. Short passwords are handled by max(missing types, length deficit), valid-length passwords by max(missing types, run replacements), and long passwords by deletions plus max(missing types, reduced replacements).
